title: iris tenax var. australis
intro: Iris tenax var. australis, also known as the Austral Iris, is a subspecies of the Iris tenax plant, native to the coastal regions of the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Iris tenax var. australis include its potential to alleviate congestion, reduce fever, and soothe irritated skin and mucous membranes. In herbal preparations, the plant's root is often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
